title: Codex Persistent Goals and Automatic Continuation · Recovery Bay
summary: Operate a long Codex task with a persistent goal, checkpoints, and user control. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Codex persistent-goal operating playbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set, view, edit, pause, resume, and clear a Codex persistent goal with the documented goal interface.
Keep long instructions in a referenced file while the goal remains concise.
Design automatic continuation around verified progress and safe terminal conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Goal Handoff evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Goal Handoff assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Preserve goal state, decisions, tests, and next actions across sessions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Preserve authority, safety, accessibility, traceability, and stakeholder consequences alongside technical correctness.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops persistent goals. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Goal Handoff, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a Codex persistent-goal operating playbook.
